title_en: The Lost Idealism of Heartland Rock
title_zh: 美国中部摇滚理想主义的失落
rubric_en: The genre of Bob Seger and John Mellencamp reached across the ideological spectrum in a way that seems unimaginable today.
rubric_zh: 鲍勃·西格和约翰·梅伦坎普所在的这种流派曾跨越意识形态光谱，以一种今天几乎难以想象的方式吸引着听众。

paragraphs.en: What was heartland rock? Did anyone ever really know? No less an authority than John Mellencamp dismissed the term as the work of “lazy journalists.” But in the 1980s, the music’s heyday, the phrase denoted an array of artists and tendencies while also conjuring something more atmospheric. Everything about the sound was big: the guitars, the drums, the voices, the choruses tailor-made to be shouted along to at a stadium or at a wedding or in your car, nowadays probably to the embarrassment of your kids (or maybe grandkids). The bigness is in the blaring synthesizer riff that opens Bruce Springsteen’s 1984 smash “Born in the U.S.A.,” and the soaring refrain of Tom Petty’s 1989 classic “Free Fallin’,” and the pounding drums and crunching guitar that propel Mellencamp’s own 1982 chestnut “Hurts So Good.”
paragraphs.zh: 什么是“心灵家园摇滚”（heartland rock）？有人真的了解过吗？就连约翰·梅伦坎普这样的老牌人物都把这个词斥为“懒惰记者”的产物。然而在20世纪80年代，这种音乐的鼎盛时期，这个说法既涵盖了一批不同的艺术家与倾向，也唤起了一种更具氛围感的画面。其声音的每一处都很“宏大”：吉他、鼓点、嗓音、以及为体育场、婚礼现场、或你车里跟着大声唱出来而量身打造的副歌，如今可能还会让你的孩子（或也许是孙子）感到尴尬。那种“宏大”感体现在布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀1984年大热曲《Born in the U.S.A.》开头刺耳的合成器riff中，也体现在汤姆·佩蒂1989年经典《Free Fallin’》高亢的反复吟唱中，还体现在梅伦坎普自己1982年的代表作《Hurts So Good》中砸耳的鼓点与挤压感十足的吉他里。

paragraphs.en: Heartland rock had a big heart, too. It was concerned with the lives of everyday people, building worlds so vivid that listeners effortlessly recognized themselves in them. Heartland rock’s imagined community lies in both the quirky detail of chili dogs outside the Tastee Freez in Mellencamp’s “Jack & Diane” and the more impressionistic evocations of bygone back-seat dalliances in Bob Seger’s “Against the Wind.”
paragraphs.zh: 心灵家园摇滚也有一颗“大心脏”。它关注普通人的生活，构建出如此生动的世界，以致听众可以轻松地在其中认出自己。心灵家园摇滚所构想的共同体，既存在于梅伦坎普《Jack & Diane》中“味达美味品尝店（Tastee Freez）外卖辣椒热狗”这一离奇细节，也存在于鲍勃·西格《Against the Wind》里更具印象主义色彩、对逝去后座暧昧往事的唤起之中。

paragraphs.en: The dominant mood was obsessive nostalgia: A remarkable amount of the music consists of grown men singing about teenagers. Tugging on the heartstrings can be a cheap trick in the hands of the wrong songwriter, but the genre’s best songs are redeemed by a fastidious eye and a poetic ear. In Springsteen’s “Born to Run,” lovers, like fast cars, are “sprung from cages on Highway 9 / Chrome-wheeled, fuel-injected, and steppin’ out over the line.” Seger, in “Night Moves,” evokes callow summer fumblings in language that’s decidedly unromantic: “I used her, she used me, but neither one cared / We were getting our share.”
paragraphs.zh: 这种风格的主导情绪是近乎执着的怀旧：惊人地，大量作品都由成年男子在唱关于青少年。情绪煽动在不合适的词作者手中可能是一招廉价手段，但这一流派中的佳作，因其细致的凝视和诗性的听觉而得以自我救赎。在斯普林斯汀的《Born to Run》里，情人像快车一样“从9号公路的牢笼里冲出来，镀铬轮毂、喷射燃油、踹过界线”。西格在《Night Moves》中用一种明显不浪漫的措辞唤起了青涩夏日的笨拙碰撞：“I used her, she used me, but neither one cared / We were getting our share.”

paragraphs.en: Heartland rock had a consistently left-leaning politics, although this aspect has long been a point of misunderstanding. “Born in the U.S.A.” expresses a Vietnam veteran’s disillusionment with the country that sent him to war and then abandoned him; the title refrain is meant to be ironic. Just a few months after the song’s release, the conservative columnist George Will chose not to hear it that way, describing the chorus as a “grand, cheerful affirmation.” Ronald Reagan praised the song’s “message of hope” during a reelection-campaign stop in New Jersey. Reagan’s people reportedly also wanted to turn Mellencamp’s “Pink Houses” into a theme song. Mellencamp intended the song to address racial and economic inequality—its refrain, “Ain’t that America,” is at least partly sardonic.
paragraphs.zh: 心灵家园摇滚一贯偏向左翼立场，尽管这一点长期以来常被误解。《Born in the U.S.A.》表达了越战老兵对自己那个人生去向之国的幻灭——那是一个派他去打仗、后来又抛弃他的国家；歌曲副歌的标题本身就是讽刺的。歌曲发布仅几个月后，保守派专栏作家乔治·威尔便选择不这样理解它，将副歌解读为“宏伟、欢快的宣誓”。里根在新泽西一次连任竞选巡演中赞扬了这首歌的“希望信息”。据报道，里根阵营的人还想把梅伦坎普的《Pink Houses》改编成主题曲。梅伦坎普写这首歌本意是聚焦种族与经济不平等；其中反复出现的句子“Isn’t that America”至少部分带有讽刺意味。

paragraphs.en: Mellencamp quashed the Reagan camp’s effort, but decades later such unauthorized uses keep occurring. In 2022, Petty’s estate wrote a cease-and-desist letter to Kari Lake, who included his song “I Won’t Back Down” in a video she posted on social media when she was refusing to concede the Arizona governor’s race. Donald Trump, who rose to political prominence questioning the citizenship of Barack Obama, used “Born in the U.S.A.” at rallies during his 2016 presidential run. The chorus of a song filled with indelible images of American decline—
paragraphs.zh: 梅尔南坎普阻止了里根阵营的尝试，但数十年后，这类未经授权的使用仍在不断发生。2022年，汤姆·派蒂的遗产管理方给卡丽·莱克（Kari Lake）发出了停止侵权函。她在拒绝承认亚利桑那州州长选举败选结果时，在社交媒体发布了一段视频，其中包含了他的《I Won’t Back Down》这首歌。唐纳德·特朗普——这位以质疑巴拉克·奥巴马公民身份而走向政治舞台的人——在2016年总统竞选期间的集会上使用了《Born in the U.S.A.》。这首充满美国衰落无法磨灭意象的歌曲副歌——

paragraphs.en: Down in the shadow of the penitentiary
paragraphs.zh: 在监狱阴影的深处

paragraphs.en: Out by the gas fires of the refinery
paragraphs.zh: 在炼油厂的煤气火光旁

paragraphs.en: I’m 10 years burnin’ down the road
paragraphs.zh: 我正沿着公路燃烧了十年

paragraphs.en: Nowhere to run, ain’t got nowhere to go
paragraphs.zh: 无处可逃，也没有地方可去。

paragraphs.en: —was pressed into service to score nativist points.
paragraphs.zh: ——这句被拿来用作博取本土主义好感的工具。

paragraphs.en: In her new history, Won’t Back Down: Heartland Rock and the Fight for America, the music journalist Erin Osmon sets out to recover heartland rock’s progressive potency, and to illuminate how the musicians behind the genre dedicated themselves to improving the lot of ordinary Americans. Although most heartland rockers emerged in the 1970s and flourished in the 1980s, they were deeply influenced by the legacy of the 1960s, inheriting from those years a romantic belief in rock and roll as a force for psychic and social transformation. In the second half of the decade, rock festivals such as Woodstock and Monterey came to be seen as movement happenings as much as musical ones, and stars including Bob Dylan and the Beatles were cast as generational spokespeople whether they liked it or not. When nearly half a million people descended on Bethel, New York, in August 1969, a lot of them earnestly believed that the music they listened to could be a key to a better future.
paragraphs.zh: 在她的新著《Won’t Back Down：心灵地带摇滚与美国的斗争》中，音乐记者艾琳·奥斯蒙（Erin Osmon）试图重建心灵地带摇滚（heartland rock）的进步力量，并阐明该流派背后的音乐人如何致力于改善普通美国人的处境。尽管大多数心灵地带摇滚歌手出道于20世纪70年代并在80年代盛行，他们仍深受60年代遗产的影响，从那个年代继承了浪漫主义式的信念：认为摇滚乐是一种能够带来心理与社会变革的力量。在20世纪60年代后期，伍德斯托克和蒙特雷（Monterey）等摇滚节不仅被视为音乐活动，也被视为社会运动事件，鲍勃·迪伦、披头士等明星不由自主地被塑造成一代人的代言人。不管他们是否乐意，都是如此。1969年8月，当近五十万人涌入纽约州贝塞尔（Bethel）时，许多人真诚地相信，自己聆听的音乐可能是通往更美好未来的关键。

paragraphs.en: By the time that Springsteen burst into superstardom with the 1975 album Born to Run, that dream had mostly died, its demise brought about (depending on the telling) by the Rolling Stones’ Altamont disaster, or the overdoses of Jimi Hendrix and Janis Joplin and Jim Morrison, or maybe just Led Zeppelin. This might explain why so much early coverage hailed the young Boss as a savior. “I saw rock and roll future and its name is Bruce Springsteen,” the critic Jon Landau gushed after taking in a Springsteen show in 1974. (Shortly after writing those famous words, Landau put away his typewriter to co-produce Born to Run, ultimately becoming Springsteen’s manager.) At the zenith of heartland rock’s popularity, it maintained its connection to ’60s-era utopianism, even as the country’s rightward lurch imperiled such bleeding-heart optimism. (The dominant rock genre of the 1990s, grunge, would take a far more cynical view of the world.)
paragraphs.zh: 到布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀凭1975年专辑《Born to Run》一举成名、成为超巨星时，这个梦想大多已死，其消逝在某种程度上要归因于滚石乐队在奥尔塔芒特（Altamont）的灾难、吉米·亨德里克斯、珍妮丝·乔普林和吉姆·莫里森的过量用药去世，或者也许仅仅是齐柏林飞艇（Led Zeppelin）。这或许解释了为什么许多早期评论把这位年轻的“老板”颂扬为拯救者。评论家乔恩·兰道（Jon Landau）在1974年观看斯普林斯汀演出后狂热地写下：“我看到了摇滚乐的未来，它的名字叫布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀。”（不久之后，兰道放下了打字机，与《Born to Run》合作制作，最终成为斯普林斯汀的经纪人。）在心灵地带摇滚最受欢迎的顶峰时期，它仍保持着与60年代乌托邦主义的关联，即便美国社会向右转的政治大势已经让这种“伤痕累累却仍抱有善意的乐观”岌岌可危。1990年代占主导地位的摇滚风格“油门/垃圾摇滚”（grunge）将会对世界持得更为冷嘲讽的态度。

paragraphs.en: Heartland rockers were idealists, but they weren’t radicals. They made big-tent music that sweet-talked more than it proselytized. It reached across the ideological spectrum in a way that seems unimaginable today, through both its sentimental songwriting and its small-c-conservative musical aesthetics: irresistible riffs, swelling choruses, unpretentious chord changes. Heartland rock, Osmon writes, was “the sound of a time when political disagreement didn’t separate families and neighbors.” That is probably an overstatement. Still, the music flourished on terrestrial radio and music-video television, two venues for mass musical experiences whose power has diminished drastically in the streaming era. The era of heartland rock was one of the last times when artists and audiences looked to enormously popular music as a vehicle for change. Osmon’s book is a welcome opportunity to revisit that idea and perhaps take it more seriously.
paragraphs.zh: 心地带摇滚的歌手们是理想主义者，但他们并非激进分子。他们创作的是面向大众、甜蜜劝说而非说教的音乐。它在意识形态光谱上的穿透力，在今天看来几乎难以想象，既来自其富有情感的作词，也来自其“保守而不花哨”的音乐审美：抓耳的吉他riff、逐渐推高的副歌、朴实的和弦转换。奥斯蒙写道，心地带摇滚是“政治分歧不再把家庭和邻里分裂开的时代的声音。”这可能算是有些夸张了。尽管如此，这种音乐在地面广播和音乐电视上曾经大放异彩，而这两个场域在流媒体时代已大幅失去它们的号召力。心地带摇滚时代，是艺术家与听众仍然寄望于极受欢迎的音乐成为改变推动力的少见时期之一。奥斯蒙的这本书恰好提供了一个重新审视这一观念并可能更认真对待它的机会。

paragraphs.en: Osmon’s cast of characters sometimes sprawls to confounding breadth, but her linchpin figures are Springsteen, Mellencamp, Seger, and Petty. Springsteen’s Born to Run is a foundational heartland-rock text, from its spectacularly cinematic opener, “Thunder Road,” inviting us to go out riding “tonight to case the promised land,” to its nine-and-a-half-minute closing track, the Jersey street epic “Jungleland.” Seger’s multiplatinum Night Moves, buoyed by its hit title song, came out in 1976. Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers’ breakthrough was 1979’s Damn the Torpedoes, which included the future classics “Refugee” and “Don’t Do Me Like That.” Mellencamp achieved megastardom in 1982 with American Fool and its singles “Jack & Diane” and “Hurts So Good.”
paragraphs.zh: 奥斯蒙的“人物群像”有时广到令人困惑，但她的核心人物是斯普林斯汀、梅伦坎普、塞格和彼蒂。斯普林斯汀的《Born to Run》是心地带摇滚的奠基性文本，从其雄伟如电影开场的“Thunder Road”开始，邀请我们“今晚扬鞭起身，去探寻应许之地”，到九分半钟的收束曲目《Jungleland》这一杰克赛德式街景史诗。塞格的多白金专辑《Night Moves》在1976年发行，并借由其同名热单走红。汤姆·彼蒂与心碎乐队的突破作则是1979年的《Damn the Torpedoes》，其中收录了后来成为经典的《Refugee》和《Don’t Do Me Like That》。梅伦坎普在1982年凭《American Fool》及其单曲《Jack & Diane》和《Hurts So Good》实现了超大红极一时。

paragraphs.en: Heartland rock tended to be more circumspect than the socially conscious pop of the previous decades. Certain heartland rockers—Mellencamp especially—did take on issues of race, but little in the music’s canon approaches the righteous indignation of earlier anti-racist screeds, such as Neil Young’s “Southern Man” and “Alabama,” and the music’s artists and audiences were overwhelmingly white. (An old joke has it that Clarence Clemons, the late saxophone player for Springsteen’s E Street Band, was the only Black person who’s ever attended a Springsteen concert.) Osmon notes that critics called the music working-class rock before heartland rock came into vogue; the terms are sometimes treated as interchangeable, but heartland rock is in much wider use. Maybe that’s because the “working class” in these songs didn’t really resemble the nation’s actual working class: Heartland rock has a lot to say about white kids driving cars (or fooling around in the back seat of them), but not much about Black teenagers slinging burgers. And it wasn’t always clear that the subjects of the songs were working-class at all. The protagonist of Seger’s “Against the Wind” is pretty bummed out, but the song’s line about his “deadlines and commitments” has always struck me as white-collar, and you’d be hard-pressed to find a line in “Jack & Diane” that says they’re from the wrong side of the tracks.
paragraphs.zh: 心地带摇滚通常比前几个十年更为审慎，相比之下，那些更具社会良知的流行音乐更锋利。某些心地带歌手——尤其是梅伦坎普——确实触及了种族议题，但这类作品里很少有像尼尔·杨的《Southern Man》和《Alabama》那样充满正义愤慨的反种族主义呐喊，且这类音乐的艺术家和听众也几乎全是白人。（有个老笑话说，斯普林斯汀的“E Street乐队”里那位已故萨克斯手克拉伦斯·克莱蒙斯，是有史以来在斯普林斯汀演唱会中唯一到场的黑人。）奥斯蒙指出，批评界在“心地带摇滚”流行前，就已把这类音乐称作工人阶级摇滚；这两个术语有时被视为可互换，但“心地带摇滚”显然更常被使用。也许原因在于，歌曲中的“工人阶级”并不真正像国家真实的工人阶层：心地带摇滚对的是很多关于白人孩子开车（或在后座乱玩一番）的唱叙，却很少涉及在快餐店忙着翻烤饼的黑人青少年。并且也并不总是清晰，这些歌曲的主人公是否真的出身工人阶层。塞格《Against the Wind》里的主人公当然很郁闷，但那句“deadlines and commitments”一直让我觉得更像白领；要在《Jack & Diane》里找一句明确说他们来自“铁轨那边的人”的句子也不容易。

paragraphs.en: For an exemplary heartland-rock anthem, one could do worse than the song that Osmon chose for her book’s title, Petty’s 1989 hit “I Won’t Back Down.” Its opening verses are carefully understated, set to a cozy bed of keyboard pads and churning rhythm guitar, but once we hit the chorus, the recording explodes. There are soaring layers of harmony vocals, a glistening 12-string-guitar part, driving drum fills that speed the song forward. And Petty’s voice is direct and instantly unforgettable: “Hey, baby / There ain’t no easy way out,” he belts. “I will stand my ground / And I won’t back down.” As a fist-pumping paean to resolve, it’s tough to beat.
paragraphs.zh: 要说一首典型的心地带摇滚颂歌，挑奥斯蒙为书名选的彼蒂1989年成名曲《I Won’t Back Down》绝对不差。其开头的几句节制克制，铺在温和舒适的键盘铺底和带动节拍的吉他上，而一旦进入副歌，整段录音便如同爆发。高亢起伏的人声和声层层叠加，闪亮的12弦吉他段，推动歌曲前进的鼓点填充，让节奏不断加速。彼蒂的嗓音直接、瞬间难忘：“嘿，宝贝 / 没有轻松的退路可走，”他慷慨激昂地喊道。“我会站好我的立场 / 我不会后退。”作为一首鼓动握拳呐喊意志的赞歌，很难说有什么能超越它。

paragraphs.en: But what is “I Won’t Back Down” about? Damned if I know: The song is incredibly vague. The most detail that we get about the song’s narrator is that he lives “in a world that keeps on pushin’ me around.” We could be listening to a left-wing protester standing up to the cops, or a small-government conservative raging against the bureaucratic nanny state, or a teenager arguing with his parents. Or maybe he’s simply a guy whose girlfriend is trying to break up with him, and he’s fighting to save the relationship—the “baby” that the chorus addresses certainly lends itself to this reading.
paragraphs.zh: 但《I Won’t Back Down》到底在讲什么？真不知道：这首歌非常模糊。我们对叙述者所能了解的唯一细节，是他生活在“一个总是把我推来推去的世界”里。我们既可能在听一个左翼抗议者对抗警察，或一个小政府保守派对官僚“保姆式国家”发怒的人，也可能是一个正在和父母争吵的少年。或者他只是一个女朋友想和他分手的男生，而他在努力挽回这段关系——副歌里称呼“baby”的那个人，的确很容易支持这种解读。

paragraphs.en: And yet the vagueness is part of what makes the song endure. Of course Kari Lake hears herself in “I Won’t Back Down,” because everyone does. Most great songs aren’t great because of what they’re about; they’re great because of how they make us feel. Heartland rock had big sounds and sometimes big ideas, but what it really specialized in was big feelings.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，正是这种模糊性让这首歌经久不衰。当然卡里·莱克会在《I Won’t Back Down》里听到自己，因为每个人都一样。伟大的歌曲伟大之处不在于它们“讲了什么”，而在于它们如何让我们有感受。心脏地带摇滚有宏大的音响，有时也有宏大的思想，但它真正擅长的，是制造强烈的情绪。

paragraphs.en: None of this diminishes the political commitments of the artists Osmon writes about, all of whom strove to live up to the ideals that their music gestured toward. The “fight for America” in the book’s subtitle might mean sticking up for the fans—battling a record label to keep records inexpensive, as Petty did, and playing midsize markets so as to keep ticket and travel prices down, which Mellencamp insisted on. Springsteen became an advocate for veterans, Seger for autoworkers. Mellencamp simply refused all corporate sponsorships. Globe-spanning benefit concerts and blockbuster charity singles boomed in the ’80s, and heartland-rock artists were fixtures at these affairs. In 1985 alone, Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers performed at Live Aid, Springsteen donated growly vocals to U.S.A. for Africa’s “We Are the World,” and Mellencamp co-founded Farm Aid, an annual music festival that continues to this day and has raised more than $90 million.
paragraphs.zh: 这些都并不削弱奥斯蒙提到的那些艺术家的政治立场；这些人都曾努力去实践自己作品所指向的理想。《为美国而战》这个副标题或许意味着站在粉丝一边——比如汤姆·佩蒂那样和唱片公司对抗，保持唱片价格低廉；或者像梅伦卡普坚持的那样，多在中等规模城市开演，以压低票价和差旅开支。斯普林斯汀成了退伍军人的代言人，西格则站在汽车工人的一边。梅伦卡普直接拒绝所有企业赞助。1980年代，跨越国界的慈善演唱会和大片头筹的慈善单曲一时兴起，心脏地带摇滚的艺人都是这些活动的常客。仅在1985年，汤姆·佩蒂与The Heartbreakers就在《Live Aid》演出，斯普林斯汀为《USA for Africa》的《We Are the World》贡献了沙哑的背景嗓音，而梅伦卡普与他人共同创办了《Farm Aid》，这是一个延续至今的年度音乐节，已为公益筹集超过九千万美元。

paragraphs.en: Osmon is particularly keen on resuscitating Seger, an artist who often gets short shrift in standard rock histories. A galvanic performer and graceful songwriter, Seger came out of Detroit in the late ’60s as a purveyor of barn-burning blues rock. By the beginning of the 1980s, though, he’d found his greatest successes with country-inflected blue-eyed-soul ballads like “Night Moves” and “Against the Wind,” as well as the prom staple “We’ve Got Tonite.” In 1983, his 1978 recording “Old Time Rock and Roll” enjoyed a second life when it was featured in the underwear dance sequence in Tom Cruise’s star-making hit, Risky Business, one of the great depictions of uncorked adolescence.
paragraphs.zh: 奥斯蒙尤其想让人重新看见西格——这位在传统摇滚史上常常被轻视的艺人。西格是位充满激情的表演者，也是优雅的词曲作者，20世纪60年代末从底特律走出，以一种“把谷仓都烧起来了”般激烈的蓝调摇滚闻名。到了20世纪80年代初，他却在更受欢迎的乡村味道的蓝眼灵魂歌曲中达到了高峰，比如《Night Moves》和《Against the Wind》，以及毕业舞会的必放金曲《We’ve Got Tonite》。1983年，他1978年的录音《Old Time Rock and Roll》在汤姆·克鲁斯主演成名作《Risky Business》里的内衣舞段中再度走红，这段被认为是青春期放纵释放最经典的影像之一。

paragraphs.en: Seger’s music also seems to have been less conducive to the sort of right-wing political co-optation that has persistently befallen Springsteen’s, Petty’s, and Mellencamp’s work. The willful mishearing of “Born in the U.S.A.,” “Pink Houses,” and other songs might partly be attributed to their sound: The bellowed choruses and clattering power chords sometimes drown out a finer-tuned political message. Heartland rock’s penchant for nostalgia might also play a role. The music’s evocation of simpler times when men were men, teenage girls were both innocent and horny, and cars were made in Michigan has a natural appeal for the MAGA movement.
paragraphs.zh: 西格的音乐似乎也不太容易被像布鲁斯·斯普林斯汀、汤姆·佩蒂和梅伦坎普那样持续遭到右翼政治挪用。像《Born in the U.S.A.》和《Pink Houses》这类歌曲被“故意曲解”听成其他含义，部分原因可能在于其声音特征：高亢的合唱和“啪嗒啪嗒”的强力和弦，有时会淹没更细致的政治信息。心灵腹地摇滚对怀旧情绪的偏好也可能起到作用。音乐所唤起的那种“男人仍是男人、十几岁的女孩既纯真又充满性吸引力、汽车还在密歇根制造”的更简单年代，天然对“让美国再次伟大”运动(MAGA)有吸引力。

paragraphs.en: Bob Seger in a Ford Thunderbird in 1982, the year his tribute to autoworkers, “Makin’ Thunderbirds,” came out (Jack Vartoogian / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 1982年，鲍勃·西格乘坐福特“雷鸟”号车（杰克·瓦托格安 / 盖蒂新闻）

paragraphs.en: And sometimes the music does traffic in the kinds of clichés about middle-American purity of spirit that have long been a hallmark of conservative rhetoric. Whatever the staunchly liberal Mellencamp intended to convey in songs such as “Pink Houses” and “Small Town,” the ambiguity of the writing makes them ripe for misuse. Osmon points out that Mellencamp’s music has recently been covered by right-leaning country artists, including Trump supporters such as Jason Aldean and Luke Bryan.
paragraphs.zh: 有时，这类音乐也确实流露出一种关于“中部美国精神纯净性”的陈词滥调，而这正是保守话语长期以来的标志。无论梅伦坎普在《Pink Houses》和《Small Town》这类歌曲中是否想表达强烈的自由主义立场，这些歌词本身的模糊性都使其容易被误用。奥斯蒙指出，最近梅伦坎普的音乐被一些偏右乡村歌手翻唱，其中包括支持特朗普的杰森·阿尔迪恩和卢克·布莱恩。

paragraphs.en: Still, the pinnacles of the heartland-rock canon continue to thrill us in unexpected ways. Petty died in 2017, leaving behind a body of music so rich and distinctive that it overflows a genre history like Osmon’s. His early work with the Heartbreakers had an affinity with the more cutting-edge sounds of punk and new wave; the term pop punk probably comes from the New York Times critic John Rockwell’s write-up of a Petty performance at the Bottom Line in 1977. In the ’80s, Petty’s music was certainly classified as heartland rock but was far more influenced by the dreamy psychedelia of the Beatles and the Byrds than most other music in the genre.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，心灵腹地摇滚经典中的巅峰之作仍以一种意外的方式继续让我们心动。佩蒂于2017年去世，留下了一部异常丰富且鲜明的音乐作品体系，其分量足以超越像奥斯蒙那样的某一流派史叙述。在与“Heartbreakers”乐队的早期创作中，他就与更前卫的朋克和新浪潮声音产生了亲和；“流行朋克”这个说法，或许最早来自纽约时报评论家约翰·罗克威尔1977年评述佩蒂在“Bottom Line”现场演出的报道。进入20世纪80年代，佩蒂的音乐当然被归类为心灵腹地摇滚，但它受到披头士和老鹳鸟乐队那种梦幻迷幻风格的影响，要远比该流派中大多数其他音乐都要强。

paragraphs.en: Tom Petty in Los Angeles circa 1988. He blended punk, new wave, and psychedelia into his heartland sound. (Aaron Rapoport / Corbis / Getty)
paragraphs.zh: 1988年前后在洛杉矶的汤姆·佩蒂。他把朋克、新浪潮和迷幻乐风融合进自己的Heartland音色中。（Aaron Rapoport / Corbis / Getty）

paragraphs.en: Through it all, he wrote incredible songs. His 1976 recording “American Girl” is undergoing a revival, thanks to a heart-racing needle drop at the end of Paul Thomas Anderson’s One Battle After Another, where it breathes its 50-year-old energy into a new image of teenage exhilaration and political rebellion. The first time I saw Anderson’s film in the theater and that chiming, buzzing guitar riff began pouring out of the speakers, I almost couldn’t believe my ears: It was such an audacious choice, and a perfect one. Maybe “American Girl” is heartland rock, maybe it’s pop punk, or maybe it’s just rock and roll. Or maybe it’s all of those things and still defies categorization, as the best American music always has.
paragraphs.zh: 在这期间，他写下了惊人的歌曲。他1976年的录音《American Girl》正在复苏，得益于保罗·托马斯·安德森电影《One Battle After Another》结尾处那一下激动人心的“needle drop”，在那里它把50年前的能量注入了青少年兴奋与政治反叛的新形象中。第一次在影院看到安德森的电影，当那段清亮、带着嗡鸣的吉他riff从扬声器里涌出时，我几乎不敢相信自己的耳朵：这是一个大胆且完美的选择。也许《American Girl》是Heartland摇滚，也许是朋克流行，或者它仅仅是摇滚乐。或者它兼具这些特征，却仍然不受分类束缚，正如最杰出的美国音乐一向如此。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the June 2026 print edition with the headline “Glory Days.”
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章以《Glory Days》为标题，刊登于2026年6月的平装版。
